title: Reading Excel and Other Formats
description: Import Excel files, SPSS, SAS, and other statistical software formats.

slides.title: Reading Excel Files 📊
slides.content:
Excel files are common. Use the `readxl` package (tidyverse) to read them.

```r
install.packages("readxl")
library(readxl)

df <- read_excel("file.xlsx", sheet = "Sheet1")
```

You can read specific sheets, skip rows, etc.

slides.title: readxl Example
slides.content: We'll simulate reading an Excel file (readxl works with actual .xlsx files). This code shows the structure.
slides.codeSnippet:
# library(readxl)
# df <- read_excel("data.xlsx", sheet = 1)
# head(df)

# For this example, we'll just show the function call:
print("In practice, use read_excel('path/to/file.xlsx')")

quizData.question: Which package is used to read Excel files in the tidyverse?
quizData.explanation: readxl is the tidyverse package for Excel.

slides.title: Other Statistical Formats
slides.content:
The `haven` package reads data from other statistical software:
- `read_sas()` for SAS (.sas7bdat)
- `read_spss()` for SPSS (.sav)
- `read_stata()` for Stata (.dta)

```r
library(haven)
df <- read_spss("data.sav")
```

slides.title: haven Example
slides.codeSnippet:
# library(haven)
# df <- read_sas("data.sas7bdat")
print("haven reads SAS, SPSS, Stata files.")

slides.title: Reading R Native Formats
slides.content:
R has its own formats:
- `.RDS`: a single R object (saved with `saveRDS()`). Read with `readRDS()`.
- `.RData`: multiple objects (saved with `save()`). Load with `load()`.

```r
obj <- readRDS("data.rds")
load("workspace.RData")  # loads objects into environment
```

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Always check the imported data with `str()`, `head()`, and `summary()` to ensure it's read correctly.
